attacking_style: Rarely attacks first; excels at late bridging moves (under 5km) or following decisive accelerations on cobbled climbs like Paterberg/Kwaremont, then using superior sprint to finish. Most dangerous in final 1-2km when positioned perfectly.
team_tactics: Quick-Step/Soudal deploys him as super-domestique doing extended tempo work early, then preserves him for late positioning or sprint finish. Team sacrifices multiple riders for numerical advantage in final 10-15km (E3 2021 pattern).
terrain_preference: Undulating TT courses and cobbled classics finales with short punchy climbs suit him best. Strong time-trialist consistently leading intermediate splits. Excels when races fragment on technical terrain requiring both power and bike handling.
vulnerability: Cannot follow explosive accelerations from pure climbers like Van der Poel/Van Aert on steep gradients (Paterberg 2022). Struggles in pure climbing scenarios and showed visible weakness when extended efforts required in breakaways (TDF 2020). Form highly variable year-to-year.
key_patterns: Elite time-trial capability: consistently fastest at intermediate checks across TT disciplines, beating specialists early
Bridging specialist: repeatedly closes gaps solo in final 2km then uses sprint power (UAE, Flanders)
Quick-Step sacrifices his legs early for team goals, preserving energy for finale numerical advantage
Wins from breakaways requiring tactical patience, holding sprint until final meters (TDF 2023, E3 2021)
Bonus second accumulation strategy over raw power: seized Tour de Suisse lead through positioning not climbing
Form peaks unreliably: defending E3 champion dropped on Paterberg 2022, then resurged TDF 2023 stage wins
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Tour showed resurgence with back-to-back podiums including stage win from 185km break, suggesting return to 2021 classics-winning form after disappointing 2022
built_from: 2021 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
2021 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
